behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the failure boundary is the CLI’s git-output ingestion plus report categorization, not just one pure function. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: rename detection depends on real git diff/name-status behavior and subprocess stdout parsing; in-process fakes can miss real rename encoding/format edge cases. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no AI model and no agent pipeline. Adjacent matrix: Integration vs System resolves to System because real subprocess+repo state is part of the failure mode. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because behavior text is purely git change classification with no model decision.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temporary git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI as a subprocess, and assert output shows exactly one renamed entry and zero paired add/delete entries for that file.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: crash risk comes from interaction between git subprocess output and parser/runtime handling. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: real binary diff handling (including non-text output patterns and exit behavior) is an OS/subprocess concern that stubs can mask. No LLM dependency and no multi-agent sequence. Adjacent matrix: Integration vs System resolves to System due to real subprocess behavior requirement. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by behavior text focusing on binary diff robustness, not model output.
behaviors.test_description: In a real git repo, add/modify at least one binary file, run the CLI subprocess, assert exit code is success, and verify binary files are included in reported counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: unborn-HEAD behavior is a git state + subprocess contract, not a single local algorithm. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: real git commands in a repo with no commits often return specific errors/exit codes; fakes can miss these semantics. No AI/model dependency. Adjacent matrix: Integration vs System resolves to System because exact failure appears at real git/OS boundary. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified since behavior concerns git initialization state only.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a fresh real git repo with zero commits, execute the CLI subprocess, and assert it returns success with an empty structured report (not an exception or non-zero failure).

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this validates end-to-end correctness from repository history through git command output into CLI aggregation/output modules. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: correctness depends on real commit history traversal and subprocess interaction; mocking git history APIs/output can hide integration mismatches with real git behavior. No LLM/model judgment. Adjacent matrix: Unit vs Integration and Integration vs System both push upward to System because actual repository/process semantics are under test.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a real repo history containing known numbers of added/deleted/renamed/modified files across commits, run the CLI subprocess against that repo, and assert category counts exactly match expected history-derived totals.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: staged vs unstaged semantics live at git index/working-tree boundaries across modules, not one pure function. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: only real git index behavior and subprocess queries reliably expose differences; mocks often collapse these states and hide regressions. No AI dependence and no multi-step agentic flow. Adjacent matrix: Integration vs System resolves to System because real VCS state transitions are essential to the failure boundary. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by behavior text being deterministic git state equivalence.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create equivalent file changes once as unstaged and once staged, run the CLI subprocess in both states, normalize non-deterministic fields if any, and assert reports are byte-for-byte identical.
